title: gibasis linearis var. graminifolia
intro: Gibasis linearis var. graminifolia, also known as Philippine violet or purple allamanda, is a flowering plant native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and glycosides. The medicinal properties of Gibasis linearis var. graminifolia have been found to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
